findings.title: Non-CSPRNG invite code generation
findings.description: generate_invite_code() uses Postgres random() which is a PRNG seeded from the system clock, not a CSPRNG. With 32^8 (~1.1 trillion) possible codes and knowledge of approximate creation time, an attacker could narrow the search space. Invite codes are the sole authentication factor for joining a group.
findings.recommendation: Replace floor(random() * length(charset)) with floor((get_byte(gen_random_bytes(1), 0)::integer / 256.0) * length(charset)) to use a cryptographically secure random source.

findings.title: IDOR in useUpdateProgress — client-supplied participant_id and assignment_id
findings.description: RESOLVED: The new khatm_juz_assignments_update_self RLS policy (lines 20-23) uses USING (participant_id = my_participant(group_id)) and WITH CHECK (participant_id = my_participant(group_id)). Participants can now update their own assignment progress; re-attribution is blocked.

findings.title: IDOR in useAssignJuz — client-supplied participant_id enables cross-user Juz assignment
findings.description: RESOLVED: The claim_juz RPC (lines 38-117) derives participant_id from auth.uid() server-side. useClaimJuz hook passes only group_id and juz_number. All preconditions validated server-side: JOINED member, ACTIVE group, PARTICIPANT mode, valid juz range, availability.

findings.title: Overly permissive khatm_participants INSERT policy — invite code not server-validated
findings.description: The khatm_participants INSERT policy only checks auth.uid() IS NOT NULL. Any authenticated user can insert a participant row into any group_id, effectively joining any group without an invite code. The invite code validation in useJoinKhatm is client-side only.
findings.recommendation: Use a server-side RPC join_group(invite_code, name) that validates the invite code and inserts the row with SECURITY DEFINER. Remove the permissive INSERT policy.

findings.title: khatm_groups INSERT policy missing WITH CHECK on admin_user_id
findings.description: The khatm_groups INSERT policy only checks auth.uid() IS NOT NULL. A malicious user could create a group with another user's ID as admin_user_id. Note: start_new_cycle RPC correctly sets admin_user_id = auth.uid(), but direct INSERT via useCreateKhatm is still vulnerable.
findings.recommendation: Add WITH CHECK (admin_user_id = auth.uid()) to the khatm_groups INSERT policy.

findings.title: RLS WITH CHECK uses OLD.role — not available in Postgres RLS context
findings.description: The khatm_participants_update_self policy uses WITH CHECK (role = OLD.role) to prevent self-role-escalation. In PostgreSQL RLS, OLD is not available in WITH CHECK clauses for UPDATE policies. This is the primary defense against a participant promoting themselves to ADMIN and may be entirely ineffective.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a BEFORE UPDATE trigger: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_self_role_change() that checks OLD.role = NEW.role when the updating user is the row owner.

findings.title: useStartNewCycle lacks server-side admin verification
findings.description: RESOLVED: The start_new_cycle RPC enforces is_admin() (ADMIN-only), requires COMPLETED status, sets admin_user_id = auth.uid() (never copied from source), and runs atomically. useStartNewCycle now calls the RPC.

findings.title: Edge Function leaks internal error details in HTTP response
findings.description: The Edge Function's catch-all error handler returns String(e) in the response body, potentially exposing stack traces, database connection strings, or query details.
findings.recommendation: Return only: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Internal server error' }). Log detailed errors server-side via console.error only.

findings.title: useAutoTracking pending queue is in-memory only — data loss on crash
findings.description: useAutoTracking queues failed progress updates in globalThis.__khatmPendingProgress (in-memory, non-persisted). PENDING_PROGRESS_KEY constant is defined at line 7 but never used for MMKV persistence.
findings.recommendation: Persist the pending queue to MMKV using PENDING_PROGRESS_KEY. Add a flush-on-app-start mechanism.

findings.title: notification-scheduler Edge Function has no authentication
findings.description: The Edge Function accepts any HTTP request without authentication while using the SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (bypasses all RLS). Any caller who discovers the URL can trigger mass notifications.
findings.recommendation: Add Authorization header verification with a shared secret known only to the Supabase cron scheduler.

findings.title: khatm_juz_assignments admin UPDATE policy allows immutable fields to be changed
findings.description: The admin/co-admin UPDATE policy allows updating any column including group_id, participant_id, and juz_number. The new self-update policy correctly prevents participant re-attribution, but a compromised admin client can still re-attribute assignments.
findings.recommendation: Add a BEFORE UPDATE trigger to prevent changes to group_id, participant_id, and juz_number after initial insertion, regardless of caller role.

findings.title: khatm_audit_log has no INSERT RLS policy — client-side audit writes silently rejected
findings.description: khatm_audit_log has RLS enabled with only a SELECT policy. All client-side audit log inserts (useAssignJuz, useAssignRole, useJoinKhatm, useUpdateGroupSettings) are silently rejected. There is effectively no audit trail for these actions.
findings.recommendation: Add an INSERT policy allowing JOINED participants to insert audit entries for their group, or move all audit logging to SECURITY DEFINER functions/triggers.

findings.title: useCreateKhatm is non-transactional — orphaned rows on partial failure
findings.description: useCreateKhatm performs 4 sequential DB operations without a transaction. Partial failure leaves orphaned groups. Note: useStartNewCycle has been fixed (now atomic via RPC), but useCreateKhatm remains vulnerable.
findings.recommendation: Wrap group creation in a create_khatm(input jsonb) SECURITY DEFINER RPC that performs all steps in a single transaction.

findings.title: khatm_progress_updates INSERT policy blocks admin override writes
findings.description: The INSERT policy checks auth.uid() matches the assignment's participant. Admin override writes set participant_id to the target participant, causing the RLS check to fail — admin override progress updates are silently rejected.
findings.recommendation: Extend the INSERT policy: add OR is_admin_or_coadmin((SELECT group_id FROM khatm_juz_assignments WHERE id = assignment_id)) to allow admin overrides.

findings.title: TOCTOU race condition in claim_juz RPC — concurrent claims can exceed max_per_juz
findings.description: claim_juz checks juz availability via SELECT COUNT(*) then inserts. Two concurrent callers can both read v_existing_count as below v_max_per_juz before either inserts, resulting in more assignments than max_per_juz allows. The UNIQUE constraint (group_id, participant_id, juz_number) only prevents duplicate claims by the same participant — it does not enforce the per-juz cap.
findings.recommendation: Add a row-level lock before counting: PERFORM pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(p_group_id::text || p_juz_number::text)). Or add a partial unique index enforcing max_per_juz = 1 for the common case. Or use SERIALIZABLE isolation.

findings.title: claim_juz and start_new_cycle RPCs do not write audit log entries
findings.description: Both SECURITY DEFINER RPCs perform security-sensitive operations (juz self-claim, new cycle creation) but neither writes to khatm_audit_log. As SECURITY DEFINER functions they bypass RLS and could insert audit entries even without the INSERT policy that blocks client-side writes (SA-012). Juz self-claims and new cycle starts have no audit trail.
findings.recommendation: (1) In claim_juz, after the INSERT, add: INSERT INTO khatm_audit_log(group_id, actor_participant_id, action_type, new_value) VALUES (p_group_id, v_participant_id, 'JUZ_CLAIMED', jsonb_build_object('juz_number', p_juz_number)). (2) In start_new_cycle, after the participant copy, add a similar entry with action_type 'NEW_CYCLE_STARTED'.

findings.title: handleArchiveGroup bypasses mutation layer — no audit log entry
findings.description: handleArchiveGroup in GroupSettingsBottomSheet (line 191) and CompletionScreen directly calls supabase.from('khatm_groups').update(), bypassing the useUpdateGroupSettings mutation and its audit logging. Archive actions have no audit trail.
findings.recommendation: Route archive operations through useUpdateGroupSettings or a dedicated useArchiveGroup mutation that includes audit logging with action_type 'GROUP_ARCHIVED'.
